title: hibiscus tiliaceus subsp. pernambucensis
intro: Hibiscus tiliaceus subsp. pernambucensis, also known as the coastal hibiscus, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the mallow family, Malvaceae. It has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to its high levels of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Hibiscus tiliaceus subsp. pernambucensis include its potential to lower blood pressure, reduce cholesterol levels, and improve cardiovascular health, as well as its ability to aid in weight loss and improve digestion. The herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal and health benefits.
